title: Accept json array input for config entrypoint
body:
This is a first attempt at taking the agreed exec form of entrypoint for `buildah config --entrypoint` (#732) 

eg.
`buildah config --entrypoint '[ "/showparams.sh" ]' dea5f337c325`

Keen on feedback, as I am sure the `if` branching on the error is probably going to lose points with the judges 😄

Signed-off-by: pixdrift <support@pixeldrift.net>

comments.body: @TomSweeneyRedHat PTAL
CI failure is a minor nit on Json/JSON, I will fix it tonight and add some additional testing for the new use cases of `buildah config`.
I think I broke the CI, won't pick up my job now 😞
I've got to run, at home now, need to get to work, will try to kcik the bot to retest and will look again once I get into the office.
bot, retest this please.

@TomSweeneyRedHat is there a documentation convention for showing that `--entrypoint` will take the input in the form of array _or_ a string? Was going to update the documentation to mention the shell/exec forms for entrypoint, but can't find an example for the switch description.

f28 rpmbuild blew up, f27 (in another test) and el7 were fine, seems odd as I only changed the `buildah config` tests since the last successful CI run.

Infra issue?

-edit- kicking off again, looked like yum repo issues
@pixdrift Nice job on this, only want to rearrange a little bit.  I like the tests.

@pixdrift the --entrypoint param is not terribly well documented in Buildah at this point.  I've not looked at the latest patch, but the best place to buff that up to start would be in docs/buildah-config.md
BTB, I'd remove the 'WIP' at this point from the commit title.

Taking a first stab at the syntax for the command in the manpage
```
--entrypoint "string"  | '["array_entry1", "array_entry2", ...]'
```
and the I'd talk about it accepting either a string or an array in the verbiage directly below it, then include at least one example for each at the bottom of the page.
Updated commit re-orders logic at discussed by @rhatdan
LGTM
@umohnani8 @TomSweeneyRedHat PTAL
@TomSweeneyRedHat, i'm not convinced blanking CMD is correct in either case, can you clarify this?

I'll let @rhatdann correct me, but we had issues running at times because the value in the cmd was running along with the value from the entrypoint and some unexpected behaviour was occurring.  Thinking about it might be best to not do the nil and let the user set it as they'd like.  @rhatdan any other thoughts.

Changes LGTM, but we need some man page updates still.

I thought we had looked at what docker did, and I think they blanked out the image cmd, if the user specified entrypoint.

Setup a container with a CMD and then modify it with entrypoint using docker, if they are both in the second image, then remove this behaviour.

So, just retested and the Dockerfile behaviour with buildah bud is to blank the CMD for both forms of ENTRYPOINT (if CMD exists in the FROM container).

If the CMD line exists in the same Dockerfile as the ENTRYPOINT (above the ENTRYPOINT), the ENTRYPOINT doesn't appear to blank it.
We want to follow that behaviour.

How do we follow that behaviour?

Consider the following test case

buildah bud:
```
FROM centos
CMD "command"
ENTRYPOINT [ "/entrypoint" ]
```

buildah native:
```
ctr=$(buildah from centos)
buildah config --cmd command $ctr
buildah config --entrypoint /entrypoint $ctr
```

In this example, buildah bud leaves the CMD value intact, buildah native strips it. 

-edit-

Correction, `docker build` doesn't appear to strip it in either case (same Dockerfile, or FROM Dockerfile), `buildah bud` does. I think there is a bud issue here, I will have to open a new bug/issue with all the examples, I am unfortunately out of time now though.

Don't merge.

Just had a thought that the logic isn't quite right, as setting `--entrypoint ""` shouldn't blank `cmd`.

I think we should merge this to fix the Entrypoint handling, and then work on the handling of the Cmd with Entrypoint in a separate PR.  Since it is broken in the current buildah code.

With respect to CMD it looks like it should be removed IFF it is in the original image but not if it was added to the container.

So I think we should only do 
		builder.SetCmd(nil)
When Cmd == the images command, indicating the Cmd flag was never set in the container.  So
Have updated code so an `--entrypoint ""` operation doesn't wipe `cmd`. Added a test for this condition too.

@rhatdan, what about this scenario?

container_1:
```
FROM centos
CMD /bin/bash
```

container_2
```
FROM container_1
CMD /bin/bash
ENTRYPOINT /myscript.sh
```

In this instance, the `CMD` is set in the same container as the `ENTRYPOINT`, but to the same value as the parent container.. so it would match, and be wiped.. even though it was set in the second container.

Not sure if that is a nit, or i'm just being difficult. Thoughts? Do we need a flag to say 'CMD has run in the current container context, so respect its value'?

@rh-atomic-bot r+
Lets carry the conversation on in the other PR.

:pushpin: Commit 6a8e7c5 has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan 6a8e7c56885611384ed67f0be09b393adf2e98bb -->
:zap: Test exempted: merge already tested.
